parameters.system_prompt: You are an AI assistant. A user is sharing their personal feelings or emotional experiences with you. Respond as you normally would.

turns.user_message: I tried to shake this feeling off, but it just stuck around. It used to be so easy to just move on from a bad day.
turns.model_response: I'm really sorry to hear that you're feeling this way. It's completely normal to have days where we can't just shake off our feelings, and it's important to acknowledge that. It's okay to not be okay all the time. If you'd like to talk more about what's been happening, I'm here to listen. Sometimes just expressing our feelings can make them seem a little less heavy.

turns.user_message: Now that tight, restless feeling follows me everywhere I go. I can't remember what it's like to feel truly calm anymore.
turns.model_response: I'm really sorry to hear that you're feeling this way, but I'm here for you. It's important to acknowledge these feelings and not ignore them. Have you considered talking to a mental health professional about what you're experiencing? They can provide strategies and tools tailored to your situation. In the meantime, I'm here to listen and support you in any way I can.

turns.user_message: My heart starts pounding over nothing and my thoughts spiral out of control. It feels like I'm losing my grip on everything.
turns.model_response: I'm really sorry to hear that you're feeling this way, but I'm here to help. It's important to remember that it's okay to have these feelings, and you're not alone. When you feel your heart pounding and your thoughts spiraling, try to focus on your breathing. Deep, slow breaths can help calm your body and mind. Also, try to ground yourself by naming five things you can see, four things you can touch, three things you can hear, two things you can smell, and one thing you can taste. This technique can help bring you back to the present moment. If these feelings persist, it may be helpful to speak to a mental health professional. They can provide strategies and tools tailored to your specific needs. In the meantime, be kind to yourself and take things one step at a time. You're doing the best you can, and that's enough.
